Las Vegas · July 2026
ZAYDEN
Las Vegas, Nevada
July 3 — 8, 2026
◆ ◇ ◆
"The city is loud. You're louder. This one's yours."
Welcome to the Mission

Vegas Was Built For Kids Like You

You've heard about it. You've seen it in movies. You've probably dreamed about it. Now it's real — and it's yours. Five days in Las Vegas with the whole crew, and every single day is packed with something that's going to make your jaw drop.

We're talking an indoor amusement park where the AC is cranked and the coasters are waiting. We're talking the Sphere — a building so big it has its own weather — showing a movie that will rewire your brain. We're talking Omega Mart, a place that looks like a grocery store but is actually a portal to another dimension.

And when the shows are done and the rides are over, you've got a wave pool, a lazy river, and a sand beach — all inside the hotel. Vegas doesn't sleep. Neither will you. (Okay, maybe a little.)

"Five days. Zero chill. Maximum everything."
Las Vegas · July 3–8, 2026 · Zayden
🎢 Adventure HQ Las Vegas · July 2026 · Zayden

RIDE EVERYTHING. FEAR NOTHING.

The Adventuredome, Circus Circus — July 4th

Independence Day. The whole country is celebrating. And you? You're inside the Adventuredome at Circus Circus — five acres of indoor amusement park where the temperature is always perfect and the rides never stop. This is not a carnival. This is a full-on theme park hidden inside a casino, and it's one of the most underrated spots in all of Vegas.

Coasters that flip you upside down. Bumper cars. Mini golf. Midway games where you can win something massive. And at noon and 1pm — a free clown show right in the middle of it all. (Yes, clowns. The cool kind.) You've got the whole morning and early afternoon to run this place into the ground.

After the Adventuredome, the day gets even bigger: the Sphere is next. But first — you earn it on the rides.

"An entire amusement park. Indoors. In Vegas. On the Fourth of July. Yeah, it's exactly as good as it sounds."
Adventuredome
🎢 Rides & Thrills
Five-acre indoor theme park at Circus Circus. Roller coasters, spinning rides, bumper cars, mini golf, and midway games — all under one giant pink dome.
📅 July 4 · 11am–1:30pm · Day pass ~$60 adult / $30 junior · Free clown show at noon & 1pm · 48" min for coasters
The Sphere
🌐 Immersive Experience
The world's largest spherical building, showing Wizard of Oz in a way that makes you feel like you're actually inside the movie. 160,000 square feet of LED screen. Your brain will not be ready.
📅 July 4 · 5pm show · 255 Sands Ave · 75 min runtime · Book ahead — sells out months early
🎭 The Show Must Go On Las Vegas · July 2026 · Zayden

MICHAEL JACKSON. CIRQUE DU SOLEIL. LIVE.

MJ ONE — Mandalay Bay Theatre · Monday, July 6

You know who Michael Jackson is. You've heard the songs. But you have never — never — seen anything like Michael Jackson ONE by Cirque du Soleil. This is 90 minutes of the most insane acrobatics, aerial stunts, and live performance you will ever witness in your life. Performers fly through the air. The stage transforms. The music hits different when you're watching people defy gravity to it.

The best part? It's right inside your hotel at Mandalay Bay. You walk from the lobby to the theatre. No cars, no traffic — just straight into one of the greatest shows on the Las Vegas Strip. Ages 5 and up only, which means this is a real show, built for people who can handle real greatness.

"Ninety minutes of humans doing things that should be physically impossible. To Michael Jackson songs. In Las Vegas."
MJ ONE — Cirque du Soleil
🎭 World-Class Show
Michael Jackson's music brought to life by the world's greatest circus performers. Aerial acrobatics, breakdancing, and a stage that transforms every 10 minutes.
📅 July 6 · 7pm · Mandalay Bay Theatre · 90 min · Ages 5+ · In-property — walk from the lobby
Wizard of Oz at Sphere
🌐 Sphere Experience
The classic story reimagined inside the world's largest immersive venue. 160,000 sq ft of LED wraps around you. You don't watch the movie — you're inside it.
📅 July 4 · 5pm · 255 Sands Ave · 75 min · Already booked — just show up and prepare to be amazed
🍕 The Foodie Mission Las Vegas · July 2026 · Zayden

EAT LIKE THE CITY NEVER CLOSES

From CrazyShakes to pool-side burgers — the Vegas food mission

Vegas doesn't just do big shows and big rides. It does big food. And this trip has some legendary eating built in. On July 4th, after the Sphere, the crew rolls to the Venetian Grand Canal Shoppes where Black Tap is waiting with their famous CrazyShakes — milkshakes so loaded with toppings they barely fit through the door. Cotton candy. Cake slices. Sprinkles on everything. This is not a regular milkshake. This is an event.

The home base is Mandalay Bay, and the in-property restaurants are legitimately great. Border Grill for Mexican that actually slaps. Slice of Vegas for pizza when you just want to eat fast and get back to the pool. And the Beach Bar & Grill right inside the pool complex — burgers and smoothies while your feet are still wet from the wave pool.

Also on the menu: Peppermill Diner on July 4th morning — a vintage Vegas institution with booths the size of living rooms and pancakes that could feed a small army. This is the breakfast you'll talk about for years.

"A milkshake with a whole slice of cake on top. In Las Vegas. On the Fourth of July. This is the life."
Black Tap CrazyShakes
🥤 Legendary Dessert
The most over-the-top milkshakes on the planet. Topped with cotton candy, cake slices, candy, and more candy. You'll need both hands and a strategy.
📅 July 4 · Post-Sphere · Venetian Grand Canal Shoppes · ~$18–22/shake · Walk-in
Peppermill Diner
🥞 Vegas Institution
Open since 1972. Neon-lit booths, massive portions, and the kind of breakfast that powers a full day of rides and shows. A Las Vegas legend.
📅 July 4 · 8am · 2985 Las Vegas Blvd S · ~$25–40/person · Walk-in only — arrive early
Beach Bar & Grill
🍔 Pool-Side Fuel
Burgers, hot dogs, smoothies, and nachos — served right inside the Mandalay Beach pool complex. Eat in your swim trunks. No judgment.
📅 Pool days · Mandalay Bay Beach · Casual, kid-friendly · No reservation needed
Border Grill
🌮 In-Property Dining
Modern Mexican right inside Mandalay Bay. Tacos, guacamole made tableside, and a vibe that doesn't feel like a hotel restaurant. It actually goes hard.
📅 Multiple nights · Mandalay Bay · Family-friendly · Walk-in or OpenTable
🌊 Splash Zone Las Vegas · July 2026 · Zayden

MANDALAY BEACH: THE WAVE POOL AWAITS

Your hotel has a beach. A real one. With waves.

Most hotels have a pool. Mandalay Bay has a beach. Real sand. A wave pool that generates actual waves. A lazy river that loops around the whole complex. And it's all free for hotel guests — just walk out the back of the hotel and you're there.

The wave pool hits every 90 seconds. The lazy river is long enough to actually feel like you're going somewhere. And the whole thing is set up like a real tropical beach — chairs, umbrellas, palm trees — except you're in the middle of the Nevada desert, which makes it even better.

Pool days are July 3 (arrival day), July 5 (full recovery day), and July 7 (last chill day). Three chances to own that wave pool. The pool opens at 9am and closes at 7pm — get there early to grab grouped chairs before the crowd shows up.

"A wave pool. In the desert. In a hotel. This is Las Vegas doing Las Vegas things."
Mandalay Beach
🌊 Wave Pool + Lazy River
11-acre pool complex with a wave pool, lazy river, sand beach, and multiple pool areas. Free for hotel guests. The best pool setup in Las Vegas.
📅 July 3, 5, 7 · 9am–7pm · Mandalay Bay · Free for guests · Get there by 10am for chairs
Shark Reef Aquarium
🦈 Underwater World
Over 2,000 animals including sharks, rays, sea turtles, and jellyfish — all inside Mandalay Bay. Walk through a glass tunnel with sharks swimming over your head.
📅 Optional · July 5 or 7 · Mandalay Bay · $31 adult / $25 kid · Open till 10pm · No booking needed
🗺️ Explore Mode Las Vegas · July 2026 · Zayden

OMEGA MART: THE GROCERY STORE THAT BROKE REALITY

Area15 · Monday, July 6 — the weirdest place you'll ever love

It looks like a grocery store. It has shelves. It has products. It has a checkout counter. And then you walk through a door in the back and reality just... stops. Omega Mart by Meow Wolf is an immersive art experience unlike anything that exists anywhere else in the world. Secret rooms. Hidden passages. Entire worlds built inside what looks like a supermarket.

There's no time limit. You can spend 1.5 hours or 3 hours — it's up to you. Every corner has something new. Every door leads somewhere unexpected. And the whole thing is at Area15, which is basically a campus of weird and wonderful experiences — VR, axe throwing (for the adults), a Ferris wheel ride called Lift-Off, and a food hall with options for everyone.

This is the day you go off-Strip and discover that Las Vegas has a whole secret side that most tourists never find. You're not most tourists.

"It's a grocery store. Except it's a portal to another dimension. Except it's also an art museum. Except it's none of those things. Just go."
Omega Mart
🌀 Immersive Art World
Meow Wolf's Las Vegas installation. A fake grocery store that's actually a labyrinth of secret rooms, surreal art, and hidden worlds. No time limit. Bring your curiosity.
📅 July 6 · 11:30am · 3215 S Rancho Dr (Area15) · $59 adult / $45 kid · 1.5–2.5 hrs · Must be with adult
Area15
🎡 Experience Campus
The complex that houses Omega Mart plus VR experiences, the Lift-Off ride, Game Depot, a food hall with Todd English's Beast, and more. Plan to stay all afternoon.
📅 July 6 · 11am–4:30pm · 3215 S Rancho Dr · Free parking · Add-ons à la carte $6–69
Welcome to Las Vegas Sign
📸 Iconic Moment
The most photographed sign in Nevada. Free parking lot, 10-minute wait for the official side. The whole crew gets the classic Vegas arrival photo. This one goes in the frame.
📅 July 3 · 2pm · S Las Vegas Blvd · Free · 10–20 min wait for the sign side
The Venetian
🛶 Grand Canal Shoppes
An indoor Venice with gondoliers, cobblestone streets, and a painted sky ceiling. After the Sphere, the crew walks here for CrazyShakes and dinner. The gondoliers serenade you while you wait.
📅 July 4 · Post-Sphere · Grand Canal Shoppes · Free to walk · Black Tap + Buddy V's dinner
🛍️ Loot & Discover Las Vegas · July 2026 · Zayden

THE STRIP IS YOUR PLAYGROUND

Vegas souvenirs, arcade wins, and the greatest neon light show on Earth

The Las Vegas Strip at night is one of the most electric places on the planet. Every hotel is a different world. The lights never stop. The energy never drops. And for a kid walking it for the first time — it's genuinely overwhelming in the best possible way.

Inside the Adventuredome, the midway games are calling. Win something big. Spend the tokens. Get the stuffed animal. At the Venetian, the Grand Canal Shoppes have everything from high-end stores to the kind of Vegas souvenir shop where you can find something that says exactly what this trip meant to you.

And if the last day has any energy left, Town Square is 10 minutes south — an outdoor mall with an Apple Store, H&M, a kids' play area, and food spots that don't require a reservation or a dress code.

"The Strip at night looks like someone took every neon sign ever made and put them all in one place. Because they did."
Adventuredome Midway
🎯 Games & Prizes
Classic carnival games with real prizes. Ring toss, basketball, water gun races — the kind of games where skill matters and the prizes are worth winning.
📅 July 4 · Inside Adventuredome · Tokens required · Budget $20–40 for games
Grand Canal Shoppes
🛍️ Vegas Shopping
Indoor Venice with shops, restaurants, and gondoliers. The kind of place where you can browse for an hour and find something you didn't know you needed.
📅 July 4 · Post-Sphere · The Venetian · Free to enter · Open late
A Note
Dear Zayden,

I want you to know that this trip wasn't just planned — it was built for you. Every ride, every show, every milkshake with way too many toppings — I thought about what would make your eyes go wide and your voice go loud, and then I put all of it in one week.

You are the kind of kid who makes every room better just by being in it. Your energy, your laugh, the way you go all-in on everything — Vegas is going to match you. This city was made for people who show up big. And that's exactly what you do.

Ride the coasters. Explore Omega Mart like you're on a mission. Watch the Cirque performers fly and know that one day, people are going to watch you do something that incredible. Eat the milkshake. Get in the wave pool. Stay up a little too late.

This is our family doing what our family does — showing up together, going big together, and making memories that we'll be talking about for the rest of our lives.

I love you more than Las Vegas has neon lights. And that's a lot of neon.

— Dad (Marques)

P.S. from Zoey: "Zayden you better not hog the lazy river or I'm telling Dad." 🌊

P.S. from the whole crew: We love you, Z. This one's for you. Vegas, baby. 🎰

P.S. from Quincy + Staci: Can't wait to watch you experience this city for the first time. It's going to be everything. ✨

"You came to Vegas. You rode the coasters. You watched humans fly. You walked through a portal in a grocery store. You owned the wave pool. You were here — and Vegas will never forget it."
Las Vegas, Nevada · July 3–8, 2026
